carrier_description: Anhydrous ammonia (NH3) with typical purity >99.5% for energy applications. Composed of nitrogen and hydrogen. Requires specialized storage and handling infrastructure due to toxicity and reactivity characteristics.

Grid-level electricity transmitted at high voltage (380–220 kV), representing standard transmission infrastructure constraints and voltage level specifications for levels 1–3 of the European electricity grid.
Purity typically exceeds 90% CH₄; infrastructure compatible with natural gas networks and combustion engines.
